deliverables: Orchestrator DB lease lock enforcement logic
Responsive Svelte dashboard and DAG visualizer
milestones: Wails application starts and lists registered projects
Single-orchestrator safety checks prevent concurrent main operations
Real-time task DAG progress color updates work without socket drops

deliverables: Streamed logs display buffer with interactive filters
System resource governor panels drawing charts
milestones: Streaming 500 log lines per second with zero UI freeze
Total Wails memory footprint remains under 120MB

deliverables: OS-level notification triggers on task end or approval gate pause
milestones: System notification pops up on task success/failure events

tasks.description: Configure Wails shell, define configurations paths registry data structures
Develop auto-reconnecting WebSocket client hub in Go backend to supervise target daemon ports
Build single-orchestrator lease lock validator engine over SQLite database checks
Construct CLI subprocess triggers (start/stop/restart/attach/detach) in Go backed by isolated execution context
Design interactive Svelte desktop dashboard and SVG/Canvas DAG viewer panel
Implement live hierarchical agent trees explorer module
Assemble streamed logs explorer module supporting regex filtering and line buffers
Formulate resource watchdogs drawing RAM/CPU dynamic graphs

exit_criteria: Wails desktop application boots up under 1.0 second with clean project sidebar layout.
Orchestrator control executions are successfully triggered without spawning duplicate daemons.
Task DAG colors dynamically mapping workflow status transitions work in real-time.
Memory foot-print of the running client remains strictly below 120MB.

rollback.trigger: Application crashes repeatedly during startup or causes extreme process locks
steps: Revert git commits of desktop modules
Verify target python runtime state integrity using workflow CLI tools
